questions.question: How many gm of the oxidising agent gets reduced in the reaction of 65.4 gm of Zn with concentrated nitric acid? [Atomic mass : Zn = 65.4, N = 14, H = 1, O = 16 $\text{gm mol}^{-1}$]
questions.explanation: The reaction of $Zn$ with concentrated $HNO_3$ is $Zn + 4HNO_3 \rightarrow Zn(NO_3)_2 + 2NO_2 + 2H_2O$. In this redox process, 1 mole of $Zn$ ($65.4$ g) causes the reduction of 2 moles of $HNO_3$ to $NO_2$, while the remaining 2 moles of $HNO_3$ act as an acid. Since the molar mass of $HNO_3$ is $63 \text{ g/mol}$, the mass of the oxidizing agent reduced is $2 \times 63 = 126 \text{ g}$.
